related_work.title: Undecidability of the spectral gap
related_work.delta: The Nature 2015 undecidability theorem is the governing external constraint the entire paper is organized around (Thm cpw, Cor no-criterion, Section wall). The paper does not weaken, extend, or reprove this result; it is used purely as a boundary forcing every downstream claim to be stratum-restricted and hypothesis-conditional. Correct and honest use of a hard prior result as a scope-setting wall, not a contribution in itself -- but see missing_prior_art: the substantial post-2015 CPW follow-up literature is not surveyed.

related_work.title: Topological quantum order: stability under local perturbations
related_work.delta: Supplies the size-independent perturbation threshold that Theorem III-B repackages as 'condensed-openness of Gap_{d,G} on the FF/LTQO stratum.' No new stability estimate or extension of the BHM argument is produced; the paper's own Discussion 6.1 concedes 'Part III does not prove a new stability theorem.' The delta is translational: BHM's finite-volume threshold becomes the radius of a condensed-open ball in a presheaf-theoretic formalism.

related_work.title: A short proof of stability of topological order under local perturbations
related_work.delta: Cited jointly with bhm-stability throughout as an alternate derivation of the same threshold, used interchangeably; the paper does not distinguish what this short proof contributes beyond BHM's original argument.

related_work.title: Stability of Frustration-Free Hamiltonians
related_work.delta: The LTQO-equals-stability-plus-area-law characterization is lifted essentially verbatim into Definition 'stratum' (frustration-free + uniform local gap + LTQO). No new sufficient condition, refinement, or weakening of the LTQO hypothesis is proposed here.

related_work.title: Stability of the bulk gap for frustration-free topologically ordered quantum lattice systems
related_work.delta: Provides the one piece of technology genuinely needed beyond BHM/MZ: a stability statement for the infinite-volume GNS gap directly, without a separate uniform finite-size axiom. This licenses Theorem III-B's thermodynamic-gap conclusion rather than only a finite-volume one; the paper's own thermodynamic-gap definition explicitly adopts this stance rather than proposing an alternative.

related_work.title: Spectral Gap and Exponential Decay of Correlations
related_work.delta: Theorem III-C is a direct, unmodified application of the Hastings-Koma rate at each point of a profinite family. The only addition is Corollary 'pointwise-fail', that taking einf over the base is necessary for a uniform correlation length -- an immediate consequence of the rate being proportional to the gap, not a new estimate.

related_work.title: Lieb-Robinson Bounds and the Exponential Clustering Theorem
related_work.delta: Supplies the Lieb-Robinson-based exponential clustering theorem combined with Hastings-Koma for Theorem III-C, used exactly as cited alongside the uniform Lieb-Robinson velocity imported from the companion Part I paper.

related_work.title: Quasi-adiabatic Continuation of Quantum States
related_work.delta: The quasi-adiabatic-continuation / spectral-flow construction anchoring Proposition 'flow' (and hence Conjecture III-3) is this paper's construction, used in the quasi-local formulation of Bachmann-Michalakis-Nachtergaele-Sims. No new flow generator or filter function is introduced; the proof sketch cites the construction and restates its consequence inside the condensed vocabulary.

related_work.title: The noncommutative geometry of the quantum Hall effect
related_work.delta: Establishes the noncommutative-geometric gap-labelling program in which the disorder hull's compact totally-disconnected structure is already treated via inverse limits of finite covers -- the same underlying fact Conjecture III-2 recasts as 'the hull is profinite, so ask about condensed descent.' The paper is appropriately explicit that a proof of III-2 'would presumably route through the crossed-product algebra ... and a gap-labelling argument in the manner of Bellissard,' correctly identifying this as pre-existing technology rather than claiming a condensed-specific alternative already exists.

related_work.title: Non Commutative Geometry of Tilings and Gap Labelling
related_work.delta: The tiling-space / gap-labelling literature already exhibits disorder hulls as inverse limits of finite-resolution data ('finite telescopic approximations'), structurally the same content as the paper's observation that the hull F^{Z^d} is profinite. The condensed vocabulary (profinite probe, sheaf condition) is new dress on an old fact; the paper does not claim otherwise but could be more explicit that hull-profiniteness itself is not a new observation.

related_work.title: On topology of the moduli space of gapped Hamiltonians for topological phases
related_work.delta: The closest ordinary-topology precedent for the paper's central object -- the moduli space of gapped Hamiltonians and openness/stability of its gapped locus -- yet Part III never cites it, although it is present in the project's shared bibliography and is cited by the companion Part IV paper. Theorem III-B's headline claim (the gapped locus is condensed-open on a stratum) is exactly the kind of statement Hsin-Wang's ordinary-topology moduli-space framework already makes; this paper should either cite and distinguish its condensed refinement from Hsin-Wang's openness statement, or explain why no comparison is needed.

related_work.title: Classification of gapped ground state phases in quantum spin systems
related_work.delta: Supplies the independent operator-algebraic classification of gapped ground-state phases that Conjecture III-3 conjectures the localized homotopy type pi_0(Gap[W^{-1}]) will match. Used purely as the comparison target of an open conjecture -- an alternative, already-existing classification scheme being compared to, not built on or extended.

related_work.title: Local Commuting Projector Hamiltonians and the Quantum Hall Effect
related_work.delta: The 'Kapustin-Fidkowski obstruction' is invoked by name twice (Remark 'stratum-boundary'; Section 'disc-walls') to explain why chiral phases lie outside the frustration-free/LTQO stratum, but the paper never attaches a citation command to it despite the bibliography entry existing -- a citation-hookup gap rather than an unawareness gap, but one that leaves a load-bearing prior result unlinked in the rendered reference list.

missing_prior_art: Bausch, Cubitt, Lucia and Perez-Garcia, 'Undecidability of the Spectral Gap in One Dimension' (Phys. Rev. X 10, 031038, 2020) extends CPW's 2D nearest-neighbour undecidability to 1D translation-invariant chains, yet is not cited. The paper repeatedly frames the 2D nearest-neighbour case as the maximal/most physical corner the wall reaches ('the hardness is already present for translation-invariant, nearest-neighbour, two-dimensional systems ... one cannot escape it by restricting to a tame-looking subclass'), which is now an understatement of the wall's reach given the 1D result -- notable because the paper's own numerical illustration (Section 8, transverse-field Ising) lives precisely in 1D.

Bausch, Cubitt and Watson, 'Uncomputability of phase diagrams' (PNAS 2021) is directly on-topic for Section 8's discussion of the gapless discriminant and 'what the finite computation cannot do,' and for the general framing of the undecidability wall in Section 3, but is not cited alongside cpw-undecidable.

Castilla-Castellano and Lucia, 'Instability of the undecidable behavior of the spectral gap in 1D' (arXiv:2607.08686, submitted 9 July 2026) shows that the specific 1D undecidable-gap constructions are extremely sensitive to perturbation: for any epsilon>0 there is a 1-local, rank-1, O(epsilon)-norm perturbation that renders the problem decidable. This is the single closest piece of existing work to this paper's own core juxtaposition of undecidability with perturbative stability, and it directly substantiates (with an actual theorem) the qualitative claim the paper only gestures at informally in the 'Evidence and obstruction' discussion of Conjecture III-1 ('near a CPW-type embedding the gap can turn on and off in a way no uniform radius can survive'). Given the five-day gap between this preprint and the paper's own 14 July 2026 date, non-citation is understandable as a timing accident rather than a research gap, but the panel should flag it: it is close enough in subject matter that the paper's Conjecture III-1 discussion and its account of 'the wall' in Section 3 should engage with it before publication, and it somewhat tempers the claim that the undecidability-versus-stability juxtaposition explored qualitatively here is unexplored territory.

The paper does not address whether its condensed apparatus (subpresheaves on the site of profinite sets, filtered unions by gap-bound, condensed-open balls) does technical work beyond what an ordinary presheaves-on-Stone-spaces / profinite-topology treatment would already provide -- a framework far more elementary than, and predating, Clausen-Scholze condensed mathematics, and already implicit in the disorder-hull literature the paper itself cites (Bellissard, Kellendonk). None of the proofs given (Weyl's inequality, the cited BHM/MZ/NSY/Hastings-Koma theorems) invoke any genuinely condensed-mathematical technology (solid rings, derived/animated structure, liquid vector spaces); the paper's own admission that it uses only 'light condensed sets to avoid set-theoretic issues' concedes the point but does not cite or engage the older profinite/Stone-duality presheaf literature that could substitute for the condensed framing in this specific installment without loss.
verdict: Calibrated against the intended novelty framing (the analytic content is 100% prior art; the only claimed contribution is the condensed packaging), the paper is honest and well-scoped about the split, and its own Discussion says so explicitly ('Part III does not prove a new stability theorem ... What the condensed framing contributes is organizational'). No overclaiming of the dangerous kind is present: the three theorems are correctly attributed to BHM/bravyi-hastings-shortproof, Michalakis-Zwolak and Nachtergaele-Sims-Young respectively, the undecidability wall is respected throughout (Remark IIIB-scope's explicit 'three nots', the repeated stratum-only qualifications, Cor no-criterion), and the three conjectures are clearly separated from the theorems with honestly stated obstructions. Within that narrow, calibrated novelty claim, however, the actual technical content of the 'condensed packaging' is thinner than the framing suggests. First, none of the paper's proofs use any condensed-mathematical technology beyond definitional bookkeeping (subpresheaf membership, restriction functoriality, filtered unions) -- machinery equally expressible via ordinary presheaves on profinite (Stone) spaces, a much older and lighter-weight framework than 'condensed mathematics' as branded, and the paper's own 'light condensed sets' hedge tacitly concedes this. Second, spot-checking the specific claimed novel moves against the literature shows two of the four are closer to relabeling than to new packaging: (a) 'the gapped locus is open in Hamiltonian-parameter space, on a stratum where stability holds' is standard moduli-of-gapped-Hamiltonians folklore, and Hsin-Wang's ordinary-topology treatment of exactly this object (moduli space of gapped Hamiltonians) is the closest possible precedent yet is never cited in this installment despite being in the shared bibliography and cited by the companion EFT paper; (b) 'the disorder hull is profinite, so ask about descent' restates, in condensed vocabulary, a structural fact (hulls as inverse limits of finite covers / Cantor sets) already central to the Bellissard-Kellendonk gap-labelling literature the paper does cite for the conjecture's proof strategy. Third, the paper's account of the undecidability wall itself, which is the load-bearing external constraint for the whole novelty framing, omits a decade of directly relevant CPW follow-up work: the 2020 extension of undecidability to 1D (Bausch-Cubitt-Lucia-Perez-Garcia), the 2021 uncomputability of phase diagrams (Bausch-Cubitt-Watson), and -- most strikingly -- a July 2026 paper on instability of the 1D undecidable construction under perturbation (Castilla-Castellano-Lucia) that is thematically the nearest existing work to this paper's central undecidability/stability juxtaposition. The last is close enough in time (five days) that its absence is understandable, but it should be engaged with in revision. Net assessment: the paper does not overclaim relative to the calibrated frame -- it is scrupulously honest that it recasts rather than proves -- but it also does not fully earn the 'condensed packaging is a genuine organizational contribution' claim either, since (i) the packaging rarely exceeds what ordinary point-set/profinite topology already offers and (ii) the single closest ordinary-topology competitor (Hsin-Wang) and the single closest thematically-related undecidability-plus-stability preprint (Castilla-Castellano-Lucia) are both absent. This is modest, real, entirely organizational novelty, consistent with the program's own self-assessment, and it should not be read as approaching a new physical or mathematical result.
